content.problemStatement:
Chef lent some money to Chefexim. He has been asking for his money back for a long time, but Chefexim always comes up with an excuse. This time, Chef got really angry, since he needed the money to buy some ingredients for his ladoos, and Chefexim decided to return the money he borrowed. Knowing Chef's love of challenges, Chefexim gave Chef a challenge along with the money in order to keep him calm and happy.

Chefexim divided the money he borrowed from Chef into NNN bags (numbered 111 through NNN); for each valid iii, the iii-th bag contains AiA_iAi‚Äã units of money. Then, he gave all NNN bags to Chef along with two integers KKK and XXX. Now, Chef may perform the following operation any number of times (including zero):


Pick exactly KKK different bags with numbers i1,i2,‚Ä¶,iKi_1, i_2, \ldots, i_Ki1‚Äã,i2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,iK‚Äã (1‚â§ij‚â§N1 \le i_j \le N1‚â§ij‚Äã‚â§N for each valid jjj).
Change the amounts of money inside the selected bags. For each jjj (1‚â§j‚â§K1 \le j \le K1‚â§j‚â§K), the amount of money inside the iji_jij‚Äã-th bag is changed from AijA_{i_j}Aij‚Äã‚Äã to Aij‚äïXA_{i_j} \oplus XAij‚Äã‚Äã‚äïX. Here, ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operation.
Each bag may be picked any number of times in different operations.

Find the maximum total amount of money (sum of amounts of money in all the bags) Chef can have at the end if he performs the operations optimally.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the initial amounts of money inside the bags.
The third line contains a single integer KKK.
The fourth line contains a single integer XXX.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the maximum amount of money Chef can have.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef can perform two operations.

Pick the bags 111 and 444, change the amount of money in bag 111 to 1‚äï4=51 \oplus 4 = 51‚äï4=5 and the amount of money in bag 444 to 4‚äï4=04 \oplus 4 = 04‚äï4=0. After this operation, the sequence A=(5,2,3,0,5)A = (5, 2, 3, 0, 5)A=(5,2,3,0,5).
Pick the bags 222 and 333, change the amount of money in bag 222 to 2‚äï4=62 \oplus 4 = 62‚äï4=6 and the amount of money in bag 333 to 3‚äï4=73 \oplus 4 = 73‚äï4=7. After this operation, A=(5,6,7,0,5)A = (5, 6, 7, 0, 5)A=(5,6,7,0,5).

At the end, the total amount of money is 5+6+7+0+5=235+6+7+0+5 = 235+6+7+0+5=23, which is maximum possible.
Example case 2: Chef can pick the bags 111, 333, 555 and 666, change the amount of money in bag 111 to 10‚äï14=410 \oplus 14 = 410‚äï14=4, the amount of money in bag 333 to 20‚äï14=2620 \oplus 14 = 2620‚äï14=26, the amount of money in bag 555 to 2‚äï14=122 \oplus 14 = 122‚äï14=12 and the amount of money in bag 666 to 1‚äï14=151 \oplus 14 = 151‚äï14=15. Afterwards, A=(4,15,26,13,12,15,44)A = (4, 15, 26, 13, 12, 15, 44)A=(4,15,26,13,12,15,44) and the total amount of money is 129129129, which is maximum possible.
